{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_018bQKbmbAdTiEb4iBYfKfuy","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01S8gNCxnAK5jB3J8r8HEMLc","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\n\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))])\ntasks = pd.read_csv('task_001.csv')\nta = ta.merge(tasks[['task_id','complexity_score','task_type','priority']], on='task_id')\n\ndef bh_cum(ts):\n    # business seconds from origin to ts (weekdays, 9:00-17:00)\n    d = ts.dt.normalize()\n    days = d.values.astype('datetime64[D]')\n    origin = np.datetime64('2023-01-02')  # a Monday\n    full = np.busday_count(origin, days) * 8*3600.0\n    secs_in_day = (ts - d).dt.total_seconds().clip(9*3600, 17*3600) - 9*3600\n    isbus = np.is_busday(days)\n    return full + np.where(isbus, secs_in_day, 0)\n\nta['bh'] = (bh_cum(ta['unassigned_at']) - bh_cum(ta['assigned_at'])) / 3600.0\nprint('bh stats by role:')\nprint(ta.groupby('assignment_role')['bh'].describe()[['count','25%','50%','75%','max']].round(2))\n\n# peer median by (role, complexity), fallback to role\ngrp = ta.groupby(['assignment_role','complexity_score'])['bh'].transform('median')\nta['ratio'] = ta['bh'] / grp\n\nemp_stats = ta.groupby('employee_id').agg(\n    n=('ratio','size'),\n    med_ratio=('ratio','median'),\n    mean_ratio=('ratio','mean'),\n    p75=('ratio', lambda x: x.quantile(0.75)),\n    tot_bh=('bh','sum'),\n).sort_values('med_ratio', ascending=False)\nprint('\\nTop 30 by median ratio (n>=10):')\nprint(emp_stats[emp_stats.n>=10].head(30).round(2).to_string())\nprint('\\nDistribution of med_ratio:')\nprint(emp_stats[emp_stats.n>=10]['med_ratio'].describe().round(2))\nEOF","description":"Compute business-hour durations and per-employee peer-normalized ratios"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1967,"cache_read_input_tokens":28404,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1967,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":32,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6154949a-9286-44b0-9583-7de472edcc70","uuid":"463de5a1-d31b-4500-b441-74f53fa429f8","request_id":"req_011CbwFynPe6w7P7dSQgUaxg"}

<< 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\n\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))])\ntasks = pd.read_csv('task_001.csv')\nta = ta.merge(tasks[['task_id','complexity_score']], on='task_id')\n\ndef bh_cum(ts):\n    d = ts.dt.normalize()\n    days = d.values.astype('datetime64[D]')\n    origin = np.datetime64('2023-01-02')\n    full = np.busday_count(origin, days) * 8*3600.0\n    secs = (ts - d).dt.total_seconds().clip(9*3600, 17*3600) - 9*3600\n    return full + np.where(np.is_busday(days), secs, 0)\n\nta['bh'] = (bh_cum(ta['unassigned_at']) - bh_cum(ta['assigned_at'])) / 3600.0\nta['ratio'] = ta['bh'] / ta.groupby(['assignment_role','complexity_score'])['bh'].transform('median')\n\n# 1) role breakdown for the four suspects\nfor e in [281,282,222,123]:\n    sub = ta[ta.employee_id==e]\n    print(f\"emp {e}: \", sub.groupby('assignment_role')['ratio'].agg(['size','median']).round(2).to_dict('index'))\n\n# 2) catch partial/low-volume outliers: fraction of assignments with ratio>2, all employees\nemp = ta.groupby('employee_id').agg(n=('ratio','size'),\n                                    med=('ratio','median'),\n                                    frac_slow=('ratio', lambda x:(x>2).mean()),\n                                    n_slow=('ratio', lambda x:(x>2).sum()))\nprint('\\nTop 20 by frac_slow (any n):')\nprint(emp.sort_values('frac_slow',ascending=False).head(20).round(2).to_string())\n\n# 3) low-n employees with high median\nprint('\\nHigh median, n<10:')\nprint(emp[(emp.n<10)&(emp.med>1.5)].round(2).to_string())\n\n# 4) time-windowed: max rolling slowness per employee (catch \"slow only in Q3\")\nta['month'] = ta['assigned_at'].dt.to_period('M')\nm = ta.groupby(['employee_id','month'])['ratio'].agg(['median','size'])\nm = m[m['size']>=4]\nworst_month = m.groupby('employee_id')['median'].max().sort_values(ascending=False)\nprint('\\nTop 15 worst-month median ratio:')\nprint(worst_month.head(15).round(2).to_string())\nEOF","description":"Verify suspects across roles and check partial-period {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Qoa6ZJNbsW92eakyiHXPZH","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01R893RaoDeUwZruBD3qq43n","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\n\ndef bh_cum(ts):\n    d = ts.dt.normalize()\n    days = d.values.astype('datetime64[D]')\n    origin = np.datetime64('2023-01-02')\n    full = np.busday_count(origin, days) * 8*3600.0\n    secs = (ts - d).dt.total_seconds().clip(9*3600, 17*3600) - 9*3600\n    return full + np.where(np.is_busday(days), secs, 0)\n\nemp = pd.read_csv('employee_001.csv')\nprint(emp[emp.employee_id.isin([281,282,222,123])].to_string(index=False))\n\n# Independent channel: code reviews\ncr = pd.read_csv('code_review_001.csv', parse_dates=['requested_at','completed_at'])\ncr['bh'] = (bh_cum(cr['completed_at'])-bh_cum(cr['requested_at']))/3600\nr = cr.groupby('reviewer_id')['bh'].agg(['size','median'])\nprint('\\ncode review latency top 8:'); print(r[r['size']>=3].sort_values('median',ascending=False).head(8).round(2).to_string())\n\nap = pd.read_csv('approval_001.csv', parse_dates=['requested_at','resolved_at'])\nap['bh'] = (bh_cum(ap['resolved_at'])-bh_cum(ap['requested_at']))/3600\na = ap.groupby('approver_id')['bh'].agg(['size','median'])\nprint('\\napproval latency top 8:'); print(a[a['size']>=3].sort_values('median',ascending=False).head(8).round(2).to_string())\n\n# Pickup gaps: previous stage end -> this stage start, attributed to incoming employee\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))])\nta = ta.sort_values(['task_id','assigned_at'])\nta['prev_end'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['unassigned_at'].shift(1)\ng = ta.dropna(subset=['prev_end']).copy()\ng['gap'] = (bh_cum(g['assigned_at'])-bh_cum(g['prev_end']))/3600\ngg = g.groupby('employee_id')['gap'].agg(['size','median'])\nprint('\\npickup gap top 10 (n>=10):'); print(gg[gg['size']>=10].sort_values('median',ascending=False).head(10).round(2).to_string())\n\n# Load: meetings hours & messages sent per employee\nmt = pd.read_csv('meeting_001.csv', parse_dates=['start_time','end_time'])\nmp = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in glob.glob('meeting_participant_*.csv')])\nmp = mp.merge(mt, on='meeting_id')\nmp['h'] = (mp['end_time']-mp['start_time']).dt.total_seconds()/3600\nmh = mp.groupby('employee_id')['h'].sum()\nmsg = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['sender_id']) for f in glob.glob('message_metadata_*.csv')])\nms = msg['sender_id'].value_counts()\nprint('\\nmeeting hours top 8:'); print(mh.sort_values(ascending=False).head(8).round(1).to_string())\nprint('suspects meeting hrs:', mh.reindex([281,282,222,123]).round(1).to_dict())\nprint('\\nmessages sent top 8:'); print(ms.head(8).to_string())\nprint('suspects msgs sent:', ms.reindex([281,282,222,123]).to_dict())\nEOF","description":"Cross-validate suspects via reviews, approvals, gaps, meetings, messages"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2923,"cache_read_input_tokens":33405,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2923,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":33,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6154949a-9286-44b0-9583-7de472edcc70","uuid":"ab2af6fe-9a30-4e9a-9bb7-92d3739f208e","request_id":"req_011CbwG3q4ysomzAy7cHqXZR"}

<< 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\n\ndef bh_cum(ts):\n    d = ts.dt.normalize()\n    days = d.values.astype('datetime64[D]')\n    origin = np.datetime64('2023-01-02')\n    full = np.busday_count(origin, days) * 8*3600.0\n    secs = (ts - d).dt.total_seconds().clip(9*3600, 17*3600) - 9*3600\n    return full + np.where(np.is_busday(days), secs, 0)\n\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))])\ntasks = pd.read_csv('task_001.csv')\nta = ta.merge(tasks[['task_id','task_type','complexity_score']], on='task_id')\nta = ta.sort_values(['task_id','assigned_at'])\nta['prev_end'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['unassigned_at'].shift(1)\ng = ta.dropna(subset=['prev_end']).copy()\ng['gap'] = (bh_cum(g['assigned_at'])-bh_cum(g['prev_end']))/3600\ng['gap'] = g['gap'].clip(lower=0)\n# normalize by structure: role being picked up + task type\ng['gap_ratio'] = (g['gap']+0.5) / (g.groupby(['assignment_role','task_type'])['gap'].transform('median')+0.5)\ngr = g.groupby('employee_id')['gap_ratio'].agg(['size','median'])\nprint('normalized pickup-gap ratio, top 12 {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_012SEwxE1h5qh7TA5ePTDnh6","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01WDVwi8HXd3N7foTpkxR4cm","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\n\ndef bh_cum(ts):\n    d = ts.dt.normalize()\n    days = d.values.astype('datetime64[D]')\n    origin = np.datetime64('2023-01-02')\n    full = np.busday_count(origin, days) * 8*3600.0\n    secs = (ts - d).dt.total_seconds().clip(9*3600, 17*3600) - 9*3600\n    return full + np.where(np.is_busday(days), secs, 0)\n\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))])\ntasks = pd.read_csv('task_001.csv')\nta = ta.merge(tasks[['task_id','task_type','complexity_score']], on='task_id')\nta = ta.sort_values(['task_id','assigned_at'])\nta['prev_end'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['unassigned_at'].shift(1)\nta['prev_emp'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['employee_id'].shift(1)\ng = ta.dropna(subset=['prev_end']).copy()\ng['gap'] = ((bh_cum(g['assigned_at'])-bh_cum(g['prev_end']))/3600).clip(lower=0)\ng['gap_ratio'] = (g['gap']+0.5) / (g.groupby(['assignment_role','task_type'])['gap'].transform('median')+0.5)\n\ngr = g.groupby('employee_id')['gap_ratio'].agg(['size','median']).query('size>=8')\nfull = gr.sort_values('median',ascending=False)\nprint('full ranked gap ratios, top 16:'); print(full.head(16).round(2).to_string())\nprint('...distribution:', full['median'].describe().round(2).to_dict())\n\n# attribution check: attribute gap to PREVIOUS employee instead\ngp = g.groupby('prev_emp')['gap_ratio'].agg(['size','median']).query('size>=8')\nprint('\\nattributed to PREVIOUS-stage employee, top 8:')\nprint(gp.sort_values('median',ascending=False).head(8).round(2).to_string())\n\n# consistency across time for the 8 gap-cluster employees\ncand = [228,45,325,26,74,81,302,204]\ng['q'] = g['assigned_at'].dt.to_period('Q')\nprint('\\nquarterly median gap_ratio for gap cluster:')\nprint(g[g.employee_id.isin(cand)].pivot_table(index='employee_id',columns='q',values='gap_ratio',aggfunc='median').round(1).to_string())\n\n# load profile for all 12 candidates vs population\nmt = pd.read_csv('meeting_001.csv', parse_dates=['start_time','end_time'])\nmp = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in glob.glob('meeting_participant_*.csv')]).merge(mt, on='meeting_id')\nmp['h'] = (mp['end_time']-mp['start_time']).dt.total_seconds()/3600\nmh = mp.groupby('employee_id')['h'].sum()\nmsgs = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['sender_id','receiver_id']) for f in glob.glob('message_metadata_*.csv')])\nsent = msgs['sender_id'].value_counts(); recv = msgs['receiver_id'].value_counts()\nemp = pd.read_csv('employee_001.csv').set_index('employee_id')\nallc = [123,222,281,282]+cand\nprof = pd.DataFrame({'role':emp['role'],'sen':emp['seniority_level'],\n                     'meet_h':mh,'sent':sent,'recv':recv,\n                     'n_assign':ta['employee_id'].value_counts()}).loc[allc]\nprint('\\nload profile of 12 candidates:'); print(prof.to_string())\nprint('\\npopulation medians: meet_h=%.0f sent=%.0f recv=%.0f n_assign=%.0f' % (\n    mh.median(), sent.median(), recv.median(), ta['employee_id'].value_counts().median()))\nEOF","description":"Validate gap cluster: ranking cliff, attribution, time consistency, load"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3670,"cache_read_input_tokens":38926,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3670,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":70,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6154949a-9286-44b0-9583-7de472edcc70","uuid":"ff5ee08d-ced6-4f97-84a6-7d6ef6ea221a","request_id":"req_011CbwG8ohmawda3RAWXxPzc"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Q9EkDEK5w25yrTXX4YrCGT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Identified **12 bottleneck employees**, written to `/app/answer.json`: `[26, 45, 74, 81, 123, 204, 222, 228, 281, 282, 302, 325]`.\n\n## Method\n\nTasks flow as sequential stage pipelines (triage → design/implement → review → approve → deploy → verify), so any one slow stage delays the whole task. I converted every interval to **business hours** (weekdays 9:00–17:00 UTC) and measured two things per employee, each normalized against peers doing the same assignment role on tasks of the same type/complexity:\n\n1. **Work duration** — `assigned_at → unassigned_at` per stage\n2. **Pickup delay** — gap between the previous stage finishing and this employee starting\n\n## Findings — two distinct bottleneck archetypes\n\n**Slow performers (123, 222, 281, 282):** their stage durations run 4.5–5.4× the peer median, on 87–100% of everything they touch, across all roles and all year. The next-worst employee in the company sits at just 1.4×. Independently, they also occupy the top four spots in code-review turnaround (5.4–10 business hours vs ~2.5 for everyone else) and approval latency.\n\n**Overloaded employees (26, 45, 74, 81, 204, 228, 302, 325):** normal speed once they start, but work queues waiting on them — pickup delays 2.8–4.2× the structural norm, with a sharp cliff to 1.5× for the next-ranked employee, consistent in every quarter. Their load profiles confirm the cause: each sends/receives roughly 3× the median message volume, several carry heavy meeting loads (228: 170h vs the 23h median; 302: 106h), and most hold above-median assignment counts.\n\nTwo attribution checks rule out confounds: re-attributing pickup gaps to the *previous*-stage employee shows no signal (max 1.7×), so the delay genuinely belongs to the person picking the work up; and the managers in the slow group (281, 282) remain extreme outliers even when compared only against other approvers/reviewers on equally complex tasks."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":735,"cache_read_input_tokens":48542,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":735,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":43,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6154949a-9286-44b0-9583-7de472edcc70","uuid":"bcded0ad-311f-4828-abe1-265ae5ca0c48","request_id":"req_011CbwGEUxp6XtVcWTjrciHT"}

I converted every interval to **business hours** (weekdays 9:00–17:00 UTC) and measured two things per employee, each normalized against peers doing the same assignment role on tasks of the same type/complexity:\n\n1. **Work duration** — `assigned_at → unassigned_at` per stage\n2. **Pickup delay** — gap between the previous stage finishing and this employee starting\n\n## Findings — two distinct bottleneck archetypes\n\n**Slow performers (123, 222, 281, 282):** their stage durations run 4.5–5.4× the peer median, on 87–100% of everything they touch, across all roles and all year. The next-worst employee in the company sits at just 1.4×. Independently, they also occupy the top four spots in code-review turnaround (5.4–10 business hours vs ~2.5 for everyone else) and approval latency.\n\n**Overloaded employees (26, 45, 74, 81, 204, 228, 302, 325):** normal speed once they start, but work queues waiting on them — pickup delays 2.8–4.2× the structural norm, with a sharp cliff to 1.5× for the next-ranked employee, consistent in every quarter. Their load profiles confirm the cause: each sends/receives roughly 3× the median message volume, several carry heavy meeting loads (228: 170h vs the 23h median; 302: 106h), and most hold above-median assignment counts.\n\nTwo attribution checks rule out confounds: re-attributing pickup gaps to the *previous*-stage employee shows no signal (max 1.7×), so the delay genuinely belongs to the person picking the work up; and the managers in the slow group (281, 282) remain extreme outliers even when compared only against other approvers/reviewers on equally complex tasks.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"6154949a-9286-44b0-9583-7de472edcc70","total_cost_usd":1.770146,"usage":{"input_tokens":1960,"cache_creation_input_tokens":34884,"cache_read_input_tokens":414146,"output_tokens":18007,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":34884},"inference_geo":"global","iterations":[{"input_tokens":2,"output_tokens":712,"cache_read_input_tokens":48542,"cache_creation_input_tokens":735,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":735,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-fable-5":{"inputTokens":1960,"outputTokens":18007,"cacheReadInputTokens":414146,"cacheCreationInputTokens":34884,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":1.770146,"contextWindow":1000000,"maxOutputTokens":64000}},"permission_denials":[],"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"6bc35a81-ef5e-43fd-bcd0-bfde8eeb5286"}
